preFix.title: Pecan pie
preFix.subtitle: American nut pie with a sweet syrup filling
preFix.excerpt: Pecan pie is the American South's nut pie: a blind-baked shortcrust shell filled with pecans suspended in a golden syrup and egg custard. The filling bakes to a glossy, dense set with a sticky top. It is very sweet and very rich, served in small slices with whipped cream or creme fraiche to balance.
preFix.sourceNotes: Pecan pie is documented in American Southern cooking from at least the 1930s. Recipes using corn syrup and pecans appear in promotional literature from the Karo corn syrup company from that decade, but earlier versions using regional syrups are recorded in Southern manuscripts. The pie is associated with the American South where pecan trees grow widely, and with Thanksgiving and autumn harvest. British versions substitute golden syrup for corn syrup with a closely similar result.

content.text: The filling is made by melting butter with golden syrup and brown sugar, then whisking in eggs off the heat. The pecans are added whole and float up through the filling as it bakes, forming the decorative top layer. The pie is done when the outer two-thirds are set and only the very centre trembles when the dish is moved. It will firm completely as it cools, and should be chilled before slicing.

Rub 100 g cold diced butter into 175 g plain flour and 25 g icing sugar until it resembles fine breadcrumbs. Mix in 1 egg yolk with 1 to 2 tablespoons of cold water and bring together. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Roll out and line a 23 cm loose-bottomed tart or pie tin. Trim, prick the base, and chill for 15 minutes. Heat the oven to 200 degrees C fan, blind-bake for 12 minutes, remove the beans and paper, and bake a further 5 minutes until the base is dry.

Reduce the oven to 170 degrees C fan. In a small saucepan, melt 50 g butter with 200 g golden syrup and 100 g dark brown sugar over a low heat, stirring until dissolved. Remove from the heat and cool for 5 minutes. Beat in 3 large eggs, 1 tsp vanilla extract, and half a teaspoon of salt. Scatter the pecan halves into the pastry shell. Pour the filling over: the pecans will rise to the surface. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until the edges are set and the centre barely wobbles. Cool completely, then refrigerate for 2 hours before slicing.

items.problem: The filling is set at the top but runny underneath
items.solution: Custard fillings sometimes set unevenly in a very sweet pie. If the top crust is browning before the filling is set, cover loosely with foil and continue baking. The centre needs a full set before it will slice cleanly.

items.problem: The pastry shrank badly in the tin
items.solution: The pastry was not chilled long enough before blind-baking, or it was stretched into the tin rather than lifted. Let the pastry relax into the tin by gently pressing it in without pulling, then chill for 15 minutes before baking.
